# IC Reactor Full LLM Guide

Use this file when an AI assistant needs enough context to write or review IC
Reactor code without reading the whole repository. Use `llms.txt` first for a
compact manifest and package routing table.

## What IC Reactor Is

IC Reactor is a TypeScript/React monorepo for Internet Computer apps. It wraps
typed canister calls with TanStack Query caching, reusable query/mutation
objects, React hooks, optional Internet Identity auth helpers, runtime Candid
parsing, and code generation from `.did` files.

## Package Boundaries

| Package                   | Responsibility                                                                                                                          |
| ------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `@ic-reactor/core`        | Framework-agnostic runtime with `ClientManager`, `Reactor`, `DisplayReactor`, and `@tanstack/query-core` integration. No React imports. |
| `@ic-reactor/react`       | React hooks, `defineReactor`, `createActorHooks`, direct reactor hooks, and query/mutation factories.                                   |
| `@ic-reactor/auth`        | Non-React Internet Identity auth state, auth client loading, and signed identity attributes.                                            |
| `@ic-reactor/auth-react`  | React hooks wrapping `@ic-reactor/auth` managers.                                                                                       |
| `@ic-reactor/candid`      | Runtime Candid fetching/parsing adapters, `CandidReactor`, display variants, and metadata reactors.                                     |
| `@ic-reactor/parser`      | Rust/WASM Candid parser that publishes web, nodejs, and bundler outputs.                                                                |
| `@ic-reactor/codegen`     | Shared generator pipeline for declarations, reactor files, stable wrappers, and client helpers.                                         |
| `@ic-reactor/cli`         | CLI around `@ic-reactor/codegen`, config loading, commands, and schema.                                                                 |
| `@ic-reactor/vite-plugin` | Vite watch-mode generation and local IC environment injection.                                                                          |

## Install

React apps:

```bash
pnpm add @ic-reactor/react @icp-sdk/core @tanstack/react-query
```

Non-React apps:

```bash
pnpm add @ic-reactor/core @icp-sdk/core @tanstack/query-core
```

Auth helpers:

```bash
pnpm add @ic-reactor/auth @ic-reactor/auth-react @icp-sdk/auth
```

Dynamic Candid:

```bash
pnpm add @ic-reactor/candid @ic-reactor/parser
```

## Fastest React Setup

Use `defineReactor` for one-call setup. It creates or reuses a `QueryClient`,
`ClientManager`, reactor instance, and bound hooks. Set `display: true` for
UI-friendly values instead of raw `bigint` and `Principal` values.

```ts
import { defineReactor } from "@ic-reactor/react"
import { idlFactory, type _SERVICE } from "./declarations/backend"

export const {
  reactor: backend,
  queryClient,
  clientManager,
  useActorQuery,
  useActorMutation,
  useActorMethod,
} = defineReactor<_SERVICE>({
  name: "backend",
  idlFactory,
  display: true,
})
```

Share one agent across canisters by passing the same `clientManager` or
`queryClient` into later `defineReactor` calls.

## Manual Runtime Setup

Use this when construction order or dependency injection needs to be explicit.

```ts
import { ClientManager, DisplayReactor } from "@ic-reactor/core"
import { createActorHooks } from "@ic-reactor/react"
import { QueryClient } from "@tanstack/query-core"
import { idlFactory, type _SERVICE } from "./declarations/backend"

export const queryClient = new QueryClient()
export const clientManager = new ClientManager({ queryClient })

export const backend = new DisplayReactor<_SERVICE>({
  clientManager,
  idlFactory,
  name: "backend",
})

export const { useActorQuery, useActorMutation } = createActorHooks(backend)
```

Use `DisplayReactor` for UI/form code. Use `Reactor` when raw Candid values are
required.

## Pattern Selection

| Need                              | Preferred API                                  | Works outside React                                                |
| --------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| Fast one-canister setup           | `defineReactor(params)`                        | Reactor and factory methods only                                   |
| Generic component hooks           | `createActorHooks(reactor)`                    | No                                                                 |
| Method-specific reusable reads    | `createQuery`, `createSuspenseQuery`           | Yes: `.fetch()`, `.prefetch()`, `.invalidate()`, `.getCacheData()` |
| Paginated reads                   | `createInfiniteQuery`, suspense variant        | Yes: `.fetch()`, `.invalidate()`, `.getCacheData()`                |
| Reusable updates                  | `createMutation`                               | Yes: `.execute(args)`                                              |
| One imperative component call API | `useActorMethod`                               | No                                                                 |
| Larger `.did`-driven apps         | `@ic-reactor/vite-plugin` or `@ic-reactor/cli` | Generated factory objects expose imperative helpers                |

## Inside React vs Outside React

Only call React hooks inside React components or custom hooks:

- `useActorQuery`
- `useActorMutation`
- `useActorMethod`
- `.useQuery()`
- `.useMutation()`
- `.useSuspenseQuery()`
- `.useInfiniteQuery()`

Outside React, use imperative methods:

- query `.fetch()`, `.prefetch()`, `.invalidate()`, `.getCacheData()`,
  `.setData()`
- mutation `.execute(args)`
- reactor `.fetchQuery()`, `.getQueryData()`, `.invalidateQueries()`,
  `.callMethod()`

## Reusable Factory Example

```ts
import { createMutation, createQueryFactory } from "@ic-reactor/react"
import { backend } from "./reactor"

export const getProfile = createQueryFactory(backend, {
  functionName: "get_profile",
})

export const updateProfile = createMutation(backend, {
  functionName: "update_profile",
})
```

Inside React:

```tsx
const profileQuery = getProfile(["alice"])
const { data } = profileQuery.useQuery()
const { mutateAsync } = updateProfile.useMutation({
  invalidateQueries: [profileQuery.getQueryKey()],
})
```

Outside React:

```ts
const profileQuery = getProfile(["alice"])
await profileQuery.fetch()
const cached = profileQuery.getCacheData()
await updateProfile.execute([{ name: "Alice" }])
await profileQuery.invalidate()
```

Prefer `query.getQueryKey()` or `query.invalidate()` over hard-coded cache keys.

## Auth

`@ic-reactor/auth` is non-React and owns `AuthenticationManager`,
`AuthClientLoader`, `AuthStateStore`, `IdentityAttributesManager`, and auth
constants/types.

`@ic-reactor/auth-react` owns React hooks over those managers.

```ts
import { AuthenticationManager } from "@ic-reactor/auth"
import { createAuthHooks } from "@ic-reactor/auth-react"

export const authentication = new AuthenticationManager({ clientManager })
export const { useAuth, useAgentState, useUserPrincipal } =
  createAuthHooks(authentication)
```

## Code Generation

Use generated hooks when canisters are large, `.did` files change often, or
consistent typed exports matter.

| Tool                      | Use when                                                                      |
| ------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `@ic-reactor/vite-plugin` | Vite app, watch-mode regeneration, local environment injection                |
| `@ic-reactor/cli`         | Non-Vite app, CI, explicit generation command                                 |
| `@ic-reactor/codegen`     | Internal/shared generator pipeline; app users usually do not call it directly |

Generated canister directories usually contain declaration files, a managed
`index.generated.ts`, and a stable `index.ts` wrapper. Put custom app logic in
wrapper modules or factory files, not generated files.

## Dynamic Candid

Use `@ic-reactor/candid` for explorers, dev tools, unknown canisters, metadata
forms, or Candid strings discovered at runtime. Use `@ic-reactor/parser` when
local `.did` parsing is needed; it publishes WASM outputs for browser, Node.js,
and bundler environments.

## Generated File Boundaries

Avoid hand-editing:

- `dist/`
- `.dfx/`, `.icp/`, `.mops/`
- `target/`
- `.next/`, `.astro/`
- `*.tsbuildinfo`
- generated canister declarations
- generated hook files

When generated hooks or declarations are wrong, fix `packages/codegen/src/` and
verify the affected CLI/Vite consumer.

## Verification

| Change type           | Commands                                                                           |
| --------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Docs only             | `pnpm format:check`, `pnpm docs:build`                                             |
| React hooks/factories | `pnpm --filter @ic-reactor/react test`                                             |
| Core runtime          | `pnpm --filter @ic-reactor/core test`                                              |
| Auth runtime/hooks    | `pnpm --filter @ic-reactor/auth test`, `pnpm --filter @ic-reactor/auth-react test` |
| Parser/candid         | `pnpm --filter @ic-reactor/parser build`, `pnpm --filter @ic-reactor/candid test`  |
| Codegen               | `pnpm --filter @ic-reactor/codegen test` plus affected CLI/Vite checks             |
| Broad package change  | `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit`, `pnpm exec tsc -b`, `pnpm build`, `pnpm test`            |
| Examples              | `pnpm typecheck:examples`                                                          |
| Docs site             | `pnpm docs:build`                                                                  |

Do not run package build and package test concurrently when parser/candid tests
depend on parser `dist`; parser builds remove and recreate `dist`.
